characters.name: Silas Adams
characters.description: Silas Adams18771876Biographical informationEye colorGreenHair colorBrownHeight5'11"[1]Professional informationOccupationDebt collector (formerly)Advisor toAl SwearengenAllegianceMagistrateClaggett† (formerly)Al Swearengen†Dan DorityJohnny BurnsAffiliationGem SaloonLast known statusAliveBehind the scenesActorTitus WelliverFirst appearance"Mister Wu"Last appearance"Tell Him Something Pretty"Episode appearances27 episodesSeason appearancesS1-S2-S3 Silas Adamsis a main character in HBO'sDeadwood. He was a former debt collector fromYanktonwho went on to become a trusted associate ofAl Swearengenat theGem SalooninDeadwood. He was sent byMagistrateClaggettto collect bribe money from Swearengen to clear his murder charges, but Al saw Adams' usefulness and had him instead assassinate Claggett for him. Afterwards he stayed on as one of Al's most cunning employees, serving him as an advisor and errand boy in the fight againstGeorge Hearst.

characters.name: Al Swearengen
characters.description: Albert Swearengen188918771876Biographical informationDied1889Liver failure[1]The Gem Saloon,DeadwoodEye colorBlueHair colorBlackHeight5'9"[2]FamilyUnknown mother[3]Unknown father[3](adoptive)Unknown brother[3]†(adoptive)NationalityEnglishProfessional informationOccupationOwner of theGem SaloonResidenceMrs. Anderson's orphanage,Chicago(formerly)Gem Saloon,DeadwoodAffiliationGem SaloonDeadwoodLast known statusDeceased[1]Behind the scenesActorIan McShaneFirst appearance"Deadwood"Last appearance"Tell Him Something Pretty"Episode appearances36 episodes(see below)Season appearancesS1-S2-S3Movie appearanceDeadwood: The Movie Albert "Al" Swearengenis one of the two protagonists of HBO'sDeadwoodas well asDeadwood: The Movie, along withSeth Bullock. He can also be considered as an antagonist, bordering on anti-hero for the series'first season. Swearengen is the deadly proprietor of theGem Saloonand a pivotal figure in the town ofDeadwoodfrom its beginnings as a camp. Swearengen was known throughout the town both as a cunning, back-stabbing criminal, and as a wise, calculating figure who lost souls would turn to. While he was a selfish man who would kill innocents to save himself, Al also had a heart and would look out for those close to him with vicious loyalty; but he expected equally ruthless loyalty in return, and would enact gruesome revenge on those who betrayed his moral code.

characters.name: Alice Isringhausen
characters.description: Alice IsringhausenBiographical informationNationalityAmericanProfessional informationOccupationTutorSecret Agent for the PinkertonsResidenceDeadwood, South Dakota(Formerly)AllegianceThe PinkertonsLast known statusUnknownBehind the scenesActorSarah PaulsonFirst appearance"A Lie Agreed Upon: Part I"Last appearance"Advances, None Miraculous"Episode appearances9 Alice Isringhausen, better known asMiss Isringhausen, is a supporting character as well as an antagonist during thesecond seasonof HBO'sDeadwood. She serves as the secondary antagonist of the season. Hired as a tutor forSofia Metz, she is later revealed to be a Pinkerton agent sent to somehow frameAlma Garretfor the death of herhusband.

characters.name: Alma Garret Ellsworth
characters.description: Alma Garret Ellsworth188918771876Biographical informationEye colorHazelHair colorDark BrownHeight5' 5"[1]FamilyOtis Russell(father)Brom Garret(husband) †Whitney Ellsworth(husband) †Sofia Metz(adoptive daughter)Seth Bullock(former love interest)NationalityAmericanProfessional informationResidenceDeadwood,South DakotaAffiliationDeadwoodLast known statusAliveBehind the scenesActorMolly ParkerFirst appearance"Deadwood"Last appearance"Tell Him Something Pretty"Episode appearances35 episodes(see below)Season appearancesS1-S2-S3Movie appearanceDeadwood: The Movie Alma Russell Garret, laterEllsworth, moved toDeadwoodwith her new husbandBrom Garret, who left the high society of New York to experience frontier life and while in Deadwood buys a claim in the gold-rich territory. It is not long before Garret falls afoul ofAl Swearengen: after Garret perceives the gold claim he purchased in a deal brokered by Swearengen to be worthless, he threatens the saloon owner with Pinkerton involvement should his money not be returned to him. Garret is promptly murdered on Swearengen's orders, only for the claim to turn out a very rich one. Now, stranded in Deadwood and dealing with attempts by Swearengen to buy the claim back, the 30-year-old widow Garret decides to try her luck on the new frontier rather than sell the claim and return East. She hiresWild Bill Hickokto investigate both the claim and Swearengen's interest. Hickok soon nominatesSeth Bullockto assay her gold claim, shortly before Hickok is murdered, which event renders Bullock the sole guardian of Alma's interests. Eventually, Bullock and Alma begin an affair, which is cut short by the arrival of Bullock'swifeandstepson, and his and Alma's mutual revulsion at the prospect of marital infidelity. Alma is, however, brokenhearted, jealous, and furious with both Bullock and his wife Martha, although she knows that feeling is irrational. Her mood is not aided by the revelation that she has become pregnant by Bullock.

Now, stranded in Deadwood and dealing with attempts by Swearengen to buy the claim back, the 30-year-old widow Garret decides to try her luck on the new frontier rather than sell the claim and return East. She hiresWild Bill Hickokto investigate both the claim and Swearengen's interest. Hickok soon nominatesSeth Bullockto assay her gold claim, shortly before Hickok is murdered, which event renders Bullock the sole guardian of Alma's interests. Eventually, Bullock and Alma begin an affair, which is cut short by the arrival of Bullock'swifeandstepson, and his and Alma's mutual revulsion at the prospect of marital infidelity. Alma is, however, brokenhearted, jealous, and furious with both Bullock and his wife Martha, although she knows that feeling is irrational. Her mood is not aided by the revelation that she has become pregnant by Bullock.

characters.name: Amos Cochran
characters.description: Amos Cochran18891870sBiographical informationEye colorBlueHair colorBrown (1876)White (1889)Height5' 9"[1]NationalityAmericanProfessional informationOccupationDoctorResidenceDeadwood, South DakotaAffiliationDeadwoodLast known statusAliveBehind the scenesActorBrad DourifFirst appearance"Deadwood"Last appearance"The Catbird Seat"Episode appearances33 episodes(see below)Season appearancesS1-S2-S3Movie appearanceDeadwood: The Movie Dr. Amos "Doc" Cochranis a main character in HBO'sDeadwoodandDeadwood: The Movie. Amos is the only doctor in the town ofDeadwood. He is an honest and moral professional who shows extreme dedication to and compassion for the sick and injured, without bias for their attitudes or loyalties. Doc Cochran is relatively invulnerable to the risk of violence in Deadwood, as he is valuable toAl SwearengenandCy Tolliverfor his medical maintenance of the prostitutes, and as a result is unafraid to speak his mind to both of them, as well as many others, whenever their behavior is cold and cruel.

characters.name: Andy Cramed
characters.description: Andy Cramed18771876Biographical informationNationalityAmericanProfessional informationOccupationFormer conman, turned self-proclaimed ministerResidenceDeadwood, South Dakota(formerly)Last known statusUnknownBehind the scenesActorZach GrenierFirst appearance"Here Was a Man"Last appearance"I Am Not the Fine Man You Take Me For"Episode appearances9 Andy Cramedis a recurring character in HBO'sDeadwood. He is a conman who has often worked withCy Tolliverand is set to do so again until he arrives in Deadwood suffering from smallpox. He eventually survives the smallpox, but was cast aside by Tolliver. Some time later he returns to the camp as a self-proclaimed minister.

characters.name: Byron Sampson
characters.description: Byron SampsonBiographical informationEye colorBlackHair colorBrownHeight6’0NationalityAmericanProfessional informationOccupationFarmerLast known statusAliveBehind the scenesActorChristopher DargaFirst appearanceDeadwoodLast appearanceDeadwoodEpisode appearances1Season appearancesS1-S2-S3 Byron Sampsonis a farmer who a horse is stealing byClell Watson. After Clell is arrested by sheriffSeth Bullock, Byron coming armed to sheriff’s office with others farmers, wanting to lynched Clell. Byron menaced with his shootgun Seth for he give to them Clell.Sol Stararrived and menaced Byron and his henchmen. Seth exiting from sheriff’s office with Clell for hanged him but Byron wanted lynching him. After Clell say his volontees, the latter say to Byron "Fuck you!" and Clell was hanged. Byron, shocked, see Seth and Sol quitting the town for Deadwood.

characters.name: Charlie Utter
characters.description: Charlie Utter188918771876Biographical informationDied1889Shot in the head byGeorge HearstEye colorBrownHair colorBrown (1876)Grey (1889)Height5' 8"[1]NationalityAmericanProfessional informationOccupationOwner ofUtter's Freight and Postal Delivery ServiceFire CommissionerResidenceDeadwood, South DakotaAllegianceWild Bill Hickok†AffiliationDeadwoodLast known statusDeceasedBehind the scenesActorDayton CallieFirst appearance"Deadwood"Last appearance"Tell Him Something Pretty"Episode appearances31 episodesMovie appearance"Deadwood: The Movie" Charles "Charlie" Utteris a main character in HBO'sDeadwoodas well as inDeadwood: The Movie. He is the good friend ofWild Bill HickokandCalamity Jane, and Hickok's sometime business partner. He runs a mail and freight business in the camp and is also one ofSeth Bullock's deputies. He is an honest and uncomfortable person with a kind and generous nature. Incredibly noble, he is also tough and fearless in the face of adversity and not afraid to speak his mind clearly.

characters.name: Clell Watson
characters.description: Clell WatsonBiographical InfoGenderMaleHairBrownAgeLate-30sOccupationPrisonerRelations(Unnamed) - SisterEthnicityCaucasianStatusDeceased (lynched)Series infoPortrayed ByJames Parks Clell Watsonwas a prisoner incarcerated in the town jailhouse in the Montana territory. Clell Watson was imprisoned in the town'sjailhousein the Montana territory for three days some time in 1876 after stealing a horse belonging toByron Sampson. While in his cell, he attempted to make conversation with US MarshalSeth Bullock, explaining that he, like Bullock, was on his way toDeadwood. Clell was a talkative man in his late 30s. He expresses regret for his actions in stealing Byron Sampson's horse and for shooting Seth in the shoulder during his arrest. While imprisoned, he attempts to bribe Bullock into letting him go free from his death sentence (which Bullock declines).

characters.name: Eddie Sawyer
characters.description: Eddie SawyerBiographical informationNationalityAmericanProfessional informationOccupationFormer dealer in theBella UnionResidenceDeadwood, South Dakota(formerly)Last known statusUnknownBehind the scenesActorRicky JayFirst appearance"Reconnoitering the Rim"Last appearance"Sold Under Sin"Episode appearances10 Eddie Sawyeris a supporting character during thefirst seasonof HBO'sDeadwood. A card sharp atCy Tolliver's Bella Union saloon, Sawyer has worked with Tolliver for 17 years and is one of Cy's most reliable hands. He eventually starts to question his allegiance to Tolliver and decides to helpJoanie Stubbsin funding her own brothel by stealing from Tolliver. He is portrayed byRicky Jay.

characters.name: Francis Wolcott
characters.description: Francis WolcottBiographical informationDied1877Hair colorBrownHeight6,1Professional informationAllegianceGeorge HearstLast known statusDeceased(suicide)Behind the scenesFirst appearanceNew MoneyLast appearanceBoy-the-Earth-Talks-ToEpisode appearances10 Francis Wolcottis a main character as well as an antagonist during thesecond seasonof HBO'sDeadwood. He is the chief geologist of wealthy San Francisco mining magnateGeorge Hearstand arrived in Deadwood in order to ascertain whether any of the claims might be of value to the Hearst empire, and if so, to set about acquiring them by any means necessary. He served as the season's primary antagonist.

characters.name: George Hearst
characters.description: George Hearst18891870sBiographical informationNationalityAmericanProfessional informationOccupationSenator of CaliforniaResidenceDeadwood, South Dakota(Formerly)Last known statusAliveBehind the scenesActorGerald McRaneyFirst appearanceBoy-the-Earth-Talks-ToLast appearanceTell Him Something PrettyEpisode appearances13 episodesMovie appearanceDeadwood: The Movie George Ambrose Hearst, nicknamedBoy-the-Earth-Talks-Toby the Indians, is a main character and an antagonist in HBO'sDeadwoodas well asDeadwood: The Movie. He serves as the overarching antagonist of thesecond seasonand the primary antagonist of both thethird seasonandDeadwood: The Movie. At first, Hearst sent one of his employees and most trusted allyFrancis WolcotttoDeadwoodto investigate the claims. He later arrives in Deadwood himself and causes a lot of problems for the town's residents with his plans to buy all the gold claims. By 1889, Hearst became senator of California and once again returned to Deadwood.

characters.name: Henry Weston Smith
characters.description: Henry Weston SmithBiographical informationDied1876NationalityAmericanProfessional informationOccupationReverendResidenceDeadwood, South DakotaLast known statusDeceasedBehind the scenesActorRay McKinnonFirst appearance"Deadwood"Last appearance"Sold Under Sin"Episode appearances11 ReverendHenry Weston "H.W." Smith, simply known asthe Reverend, is a supporting character during thefirst seasonof HBO'sDeadwood. He was one of the early residents ofDeadwood, South Dakota and served as the camp's preacher.

characters.name: Jack Langrishe
characters.description: Jack LangrisheBiographical informationNationalityIrish-AmericanProfessional informationOccupationTheater operatorResidenceDeadwood, South Dakota(formerly)Last known statusUnknownBehind the scenesActorBrian CoxFirst appearance"True Colors"Last appearance"Tell Him Something Pretty"Episode appearances9 John Sewell Langrishe, better known asJack Langrishe, is a supporting character during thethird seasonof HBO'sDeadwood. He is a theater operator and leader of a troupe of traveling actors who come to settle inDeadwood. Langrishe is a close, longtime friend ofAl Swearengen. Possessing a dry wit, and prone to both flattery and self-deprecation, the flamboyant Langrishe charms most of the people he meets.

characters.name: James Butler Hickok
characters.description: Wild Bill HickokBiographical informationDied1876NationalityAmericanProfessional informationOccupationGunslingerResidenceDeadwood, South DakotaLast known statusDeceasedBehind the scenesActorKeith CarradineFirst appearance"Deadwood"Last appearance"The Trial of Jack McCall"(corpse)Episode appearances5Season appearancesS1-S2-S3Movie appearanceDeadwood: The Movie(flashback) James Butler Hickok, better known asWild Bill Hickok, is a main character during the first half of thefirst seasonof HBO'sDeadwood. He arrived in Deadwood as one of its first residents, along with his good friendsCharlie UtterandCalamity Jane, in July of 1876.  He was a well-known gambler and gunslinger, participating in many shootouts before coming to Deadwood.

characters.name: Jane Canary
characters.description: Jane CanaryBiographical InfoGenderFemaleHairDark BrownAgeMid-30sOccupationAssistant to Doc CochranRelationsJames Butler Hickok(best friend) †Joanie Stubbs- GirlfriendEthnicityCaucasianStatusAliveSeries infoPortrayed ByRobin Weigert"Calamity" Jane Canary(based upon the historical Calamity Jane) is the traveling companion of"Wild Bill" Hickok, the great gunslinger known to all in the camp well before his arrival. She is also the town drunk. Jane Canary traveled to Deadwood with companions Charlie Utter and Wild Bill Hickok. In doing so, the wagon train is brought to a halt when one of the wagon's breaks down, much to Jane's annoyance. Wild Bill is laying down in another wagon, afflicted with a headache. Jane offers to search for whiskey, but he declines. Later on, Jane asks the Metz family if they know the way back, to which they answer they are not returning to the camp but instead returning to Minnesota. Incredibly tough and brazen on the outside, Jane has been shown to be deeply wounded, cowardly, and emotionally delicate when prodded. Because she dresses in traditionally male clothing, she is sometimes mistaken for a man. She is well acquainted with the outdoors, with her face perpetually dirtied. She speaks with a mush-mouth slur, exacerbated by heavy drinking.

characters.name: Jimmy Irons
characters.description: Jimmy IronsBiographical informationDied1877Gem Saloonbathhouse,Deadwood, South DakotaDrowned byAl Swearengenin punishmentEye colorBrownHair colorBrownNationalityAmericanProfessional informationOccupationAssistant toAl SwearengenResidenceDeadwood, South DakotaAllegianceAl Swearengen†AffiliationGem SaloonLast known statusDeceasedBehind the scenesActorDean Rader-DuvalFirst appearance"Deadwood"Last appearance"Mister Wu"Episode appearances"Deadwood""Deep Water""Reconnoitering the Rim""Mister Wu"Season appearancesS1-S2-S3' Jimmy Ironswas a hand at theGem Saloon, who served as one of ownerAl Swearengen's more dispensable enforcers. Alongside theMason brothersandPersimmon Phil, Jimmy worked as a road agent and errand boy for Al, though he was more loyal to his co-workers than his boss.[1]Along with his counterpart Leon at the rivalBella Union, however, Jimmy had an addiction to opium, something the two let get the better of them when they robbed and killed one ofMr. Wu's dope couriers. Afterwards, Al chose to kill only one of the two thieves in punishment, but spared Leon since he knew killing one ofCy Tolliver's men would only tighten their rivalry - as such, it was Jimmy who Swearengen drowned in the Gem bathhouse.[2] Jimmy Irons was played byDean Rader-Duvalin several episodes ofSeason 1ofDeadwood.

Alongside theMason brothersandPersimmon Phil, Jimmy worked as a road agent and errand boy for Al, though he was more loyal to his co-workers than his boss.[1]Along with his counterpart Leon at the rivalBella Union, however, Jimmy had an addiction to opium, something the two let get the better of them when they robbed and killed one ofMr. Wu's dope couriers. Afterwards, Al chose to kill only one of the two thieves in punishment, but spared Leon since he knew killing one ofCy Tolliver's men would only tighten their rivalry - as such, it was Jimmy who Swearengen drowned in the Gem bathhouse.[2] Jimmy Irons was played byDean Rader-Duvalin several episodes ofSeason 1ofDeadwood.

characters.name: Lee’s Henchman
characters.description: UnknownBiographical informationDied1877,Deadwood, stabbed with an axe byMr. Wu’s menHair colorNoneNationalityAsiaticProfessional informationOccupationHenchmanAffiliationMr. LeeLast known statusDeceasedBehind the scenesFirst appearanceThe Whores Can ComeLast appearanceThe Whores Can ComeSeason appearancesS1-S2-S3 Anunidentified asiatic manwasMr. Lee’s bodyguard inDeadwood. When Mr. Wu attempted an assault on Mr. Lee with his two men, one of them talked to this man. The second took an axe and stabbed him in the legs. After the death of his henchman, Mr. Lee shoot in the ground the man who killed his henchman. The body of this man was later donated to ate atMr. Wu’s pigs.

characters.name: Martha Bullock
characters.description: Martha BullockBiographical informationEye colorGreenHair colorBlondFamilyRobert Bullock (first husband) †Seth Bullock(second husband)William Bullock(son) †Margaret Bullock](daughter)Florence Bullock (daughter)Stanley Bullock (son)Unborn Child †NationalityAmericanProfessional informationResidenceDeadwoodAffiliationDeadwoodLast known statusAliveBehind the scenesActorAnna GunnFirst appearance"A Lie Agreed Upon: Part I"Last appearance"Tell Him Something Pretty"Episode appearances24 episodesSeason appearancesS1-S2-S3Movie appearanceDeadwood: The Movie Martha Bullockwas the wife ofRobert Bullockand, after the death of the latter, ofSeth Bullock. She had four childrens, including two premature deaths. Seth's brotherRoberthad been a cavalryman and died while fighting Comancheros in Texas. Bullock felt obliged to marry and take care of the widow andher son(Seth's nephew), although he is not actually romantically involved with her. Such marriages were a common custom of the time to prevent children from growing up fatherless after the Civil War and Indian Wars. Martha arrives in Deadwood at the beginning of Season 2. She feels a confusing mix of gratitude and duty towards Bullock, perhaps even romantic love for him, but wishes he not sacrifice his own happiness any more than necessary to provide for her and her son. Even so, she still harbors a great deal of jealousy towards her husband's former relationship with and persistent feelings for Alma Garret, though she is perplexed by his reluctance to act on them. Martha is devastated by the death of her son William. The grief, however, brings her and Seth closer together and they continue to live together in Deadwood. She later becomes the teacher of the camp's children. As ofDeadwood: The Movie, she and Seth remain happily married and have three children.
